item.title: [8] Sliding-driven spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in an altermagnet
item.description:
Authors: Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Directly addresses altermagnetism and Berry curvature transport, which are profile keywords.

Tags: #altermagnet #Berrycurvature #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao Here, we propose a method to introduce the magnon orbital Nernst effect in twisted collinear magnets through broken spatial inversion symmetry. We demonstrate that nonzero Berry curvature and orbital moments of magnons can be achieved by atomic translation, including spin-polarized transport of magn… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao

Here, we propose a method to introduce the magnon orbital Nernst effect in twisted collinear magnets through broken spatial inversion symmetry. We demonstrate that nonzero Berry curvature and orbital moments of magnons can be achieved by atomic translation, including spin-polarized transport of magn…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Sliding-driven spin-polarized transport of magnon orbital moments in an altermagnet
item.dc:creator: Haodong Yu, Fawei Zheng, and Yugui Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Helical phases and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces probed by superconducting diode effects
item.description:
Authors: Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces and Rashba SOC are highly relevant to topological superconductivity and ARPES.

Tags: #Fermisurface #superconductivity #topology

Abstract: Author(s): Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko Noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) with Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and in-plane magnetic fields have emerged as natural platforms for realizing both the bulk superconducting diode effect (SDE) and the Josephson diode effect (JDE)—phenomena characterized by unequal critical currents in o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174501] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko

Noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) with Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and in-plane magnetic fields have emerged as natural platforms for realizing both the bulk superconducting diode effect (SDE) and the Josephson diode effect (JDE)—phenomena characterized by unequal critical currents in o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174501] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Helical phases and Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces probed by superconducting diode effects
item.dc:creator: Zekun Zhuang, Daniel Shaffer, Jaglul Hasan, and Alex Levchenko
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [10] Josephson spectroscopy study of kagome superconductors toward the deep point-contact regime
item.description:
Authors: Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin

Score 10/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct hit: Josephson STM/STS on kagome superconductors; involves lead authors in the field.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #kagome #STM #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin Josephson scanning tunneling microscopy (JSTM) has emerged as an important technique for probing the superconducting order parameter at the atomic scale. However, the Josephson current in JSTM may behave quite differently when the coupling strength varies. Here, we push the junction to the deep poin… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174502] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hailang Qin, Xiao-Yu Yan, Hanbin Deng, Mu-Wei Gao, Guowei Liu, Yuanyuan Zhao, and Jia-Xin Yin

Josephson scanning tunneling microscopy (JSTM) has emerged as an important technique for probing the superconducting order parameter at the atomic scale. However, the Josephson current in JSTM may behave quite differently when the coupling strength varies. Here, we push the junction to the deep poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 174502] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Josephson spectroscopy study of kagome superconductors toward the deep point-contact regime

item.title: [8] Slow-phonon control of spin Edelstein effect in Rashba $d$-wave altermagnets
item.description:
Authors: Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Altermagnetism and spin-split band physics are key interests for this electronic structure specialist.

Tags: #altermagnet #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks Altermagnets have zero net magnetization yet feature spin-split bands. Here, we investigate how slow lattice vibrations (phonons) influence both the intrinsic and externally induced spin polarizations in two-dimensional $d$-wave altermagnets. For the induced spin polarization, we employ a Rashba con… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184403] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks

Altermagnets have zero net magnetization yet feature spin-split bands. Here, we investigate how slow lattice vibrations (phonons) influence both the intrinsic and externally induced spin polarizations in two-dimensional d-wave altermagnets. For the induced spin polarization, we employ a Rashba conti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184403] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Slow-phonon control of spin Edelstein effect in Rashba $d$-wave altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Jacob Linder, and James K. Freericks

item.title: [7] Type-II multiferroics in collinear ferrimagnetism and altermagnetism
item.description:
Authors: Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevance to altermagnetism, a stated secondary interest for the user.

Tags: #altermagnetism #multiferroics

Abstract: Author(s): Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang Type-II multiferroics offer a promising route to strong magnetoelectric coupling due to the magnetic-order-driven ferroelectricity. Most experimentally realized type-II multiferroics exhibit noncollinear magnetic order, whose complex interaction with external fields can impede the practical control … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195105] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Songsong Yan, Hanjing Zhou, Dingwen Zhang, Huimei Liu, Sergey Y. Savrasov, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wang

Type-II multiferroics offer a promising route to strong magnetoelectric coupling due to the magnetic-order-driven ferroelectricity. Most experimentally realized type-II multiferroics exhibit noncollinear magnetic order, whose complex interaction with external fields can impede the practical control …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195105] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [7] Berry curvature dipole induced chiral terahertz gain and lasing threshold in bulk tellurium
item.description:
Authors: Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, and Filippo Capolino

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, and Filippo Capolino

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Focuses on Berry curvature in a chiral topological material.

Tags: #Berry-curvature #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, and Filippo Capolino We investigate the use of Berry curvature dipole in $n$-doped tellurium as a mechanism for achieving terahertz amplification and lasing by applying a DC electric field. When the electrical bias and wave vector are aligned along the trigonal $c$ axis, the right-handed circularly polarized mode experi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195202] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mounes Eslami, Amin Hakimi, Luis A. Jauregui, and Filippo Capolino

We investigate the use of Berry curvature dipole in n-doped tellurium as a mechanism for achieving terahertz amplification and lasing by applying a DC electric field. When the electrical bias and wave vector are aligned along the trigonal c axis, the right-handed circularly polarized mode experience…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195202] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Berry curvature dipole induced chiral terahertz gain and lasing threshold in bulk tellurium

item.description:
Authors: Valery Ortiz Jimenez, Paul M. Haney, Farzad Mahfouzi, Ngoc Thanh Mai Tran, Albert F. Rigosi, and Curt A. Richter

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Valery Ortiz Jimenez, Paul M. Haney, Farzad Mahfouzi, Ngoc Thanh Mai Tran, Albert F. Rigosi, and Curt A. Richter

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental transport on magnetic topological insulators and anomalous Hall effect.

Tags: #topological-insulator #anomalous-Hall

Abstract: Author(s): Valery Ortiz Jimenez, Paul M. Haney, Farzad Mahfouzi, Ngoc Thanh Mai Tran, Albert F. Rigosi, and Curt A. Richter The quantum anomalous Hall effect shows great promise for the realization of the ohm without the need for an external magnetic field. The most mature material platform is magnetically doped topological insulators. In these materials, precise quantization is limited to low temperatures, with the acti… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Valery Ortiz Jimenez, Paul M. Haney, Farzad Mahfouzi, Ngoc Thanh Mai Tran, Albert F. Rigosi, and Curt A. Richter

The quantum anomalous Hall effect shows great promise for the realization of the ohm without the need for an external magnetic field. The most mature material platform is magnetically doped topological insulators. In these materials, precise quantization is limited to low temperatures, with the acti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195402] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [7] Mott insulating Peierls distortion in single atomic wire of niobium triiodide

Authors: Chufeng Huang, Xing Wei, Nanshu Liu, Jijun Zhao, and Si Zhou

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Chufeng Huang, Xing Wei, Nanshu Liu, Jijun Zhao, and Si Zhou

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevance to quasi-1D materials, CDW, and niobium-based chalcogenide/halide physics.

Tags: #quasi-1D #CDW #Mott

Abstract: Author(s): Chufeng Huang, Xing Wei, Nanshu Liu, Jijun Zhao, and Si Zhou Single atomic wires of transition metal compounds have been successfully isolated within carbon nanotubes. These real one-dimensional (1D) materials with enhanced electron-electron correlations and strong electron-phonon coupling provide a unique platform to explore the competition and synergy among… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chufeng Huang, Xing Wei, Nanshu Liu, Jijun Zhao, and Si Zhou

Single atomic wires of transition metal compounds have been successfully isolated within carbon nanotubes. These real one-dimensional (1D) materials with enhanced electron-electron correlations and strong electron-phonon coupling provide a unique platform to explore the competition and synergy among…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205401] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [9] Optical signatures of ultrafast lattice distortion in the ternary type-II Weyl semimetal $\mathrm{TaIr}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$
item.description:
Authors: David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, Wenxu Duan, Jiangxu Li, Yangchen He, Yulu Mao, Ying Wang, Daniel A. Rhodes, Daniel D. Kohler, Yang Zhang, Jun Xiao, and John C. Wright

Last/corresponding-author proxy: David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, Wenxu Duan, Jiangxu Li, Yangchen He, Yulu Mao, Ying Wang, Daniel A. Rhodes, Daniel D. Kohler, Yang Zhang, Jun Xiao, and John C. Wright

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct hit on Weyl semimetals and tellurides closely related to user's 112 projects.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #Weyl #tellurides #topological

Abstract: Author(s): David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, Wenxu Duan, Jiangxu Li, Yangchen He, Yulu Mao, Ying Wang, Daniel A. Rhodes, Daniel D. Kohler, Yang Zhang, Jun Xiao, and John C. Wright ${T}_{d}$ phase type-II Weyl semimetals are of technological interest in part due to their novel sliding ferroelectricity and nontrivial topology being dependent on their van der Waals stacking orders. Optical manipulation of these stacking orders via interlayer sliding can lead to new types of ener… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David P. Lafayette, II, Li Cheng, Wenxu Duan, Jiangxu Li, Yangchen He, Yulu Mao, Ying Wang, Daniel A. Rhodes, Daniel D. Kohler, Yang Zhang, Jun Xiao, and John C. Wright

Td phase type-II Weyl semimetals are of technological interest in part due to their novel sliding ferroelectricity and nontrivial topology being dependent on their van der Waals stacking orders. Optical manipulation of these stacking orders via interlayer sliding can lead to new types of energy-effi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205402] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical signatures of ultrafast lattice distortion in the ternary type-II Weyl semimetal $\mathrm{TaIr}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$

item.title: [7] Spin-dependent photon-assisted transport properties in altermagnets

Authors: Yun-Xia Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, and Yu-Xian Li

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Yun-Xia Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, and Yu-Xian Li

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Transport theory in altermagnets, a priority secondary interest.

Tags: #altermagnetism #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Yun-Xia Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, and Yu-Xian Li The spin-dependent transport properties and spin polarization through a time-periodic potential well in the altermagnet are investigated. Photon-assisted transport occurs in periodically driven quantum systems, where incident electrons can exchange energy with the driven field by absorbing or emitti… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205404] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yun-Xia Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, and Yu-Xian Li

The spin-dependent transport properties and spin polarization through a time-periodic potential well in the altermagnet are investigated. Photon-assisted transport occurs in periodically driven quantum systems, where incident electrons can exchange energy with the driven field by absorbing or emitti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205404] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [8] Large anomalous Hall effect in double-Q magnets without net scalar spin chirality
item.description:
Authors: Satoru Ohgata and Satoru Hayami

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Satoru Ohgata and Satoru Hayami

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Significant for user's interest in anomalous Hall effect and non-trivial magnetic orders.

Tags: #anomalous-Hall #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Satoru Ohgata and Satoru Hayami Multi-Q magnets consist of superposed spin-density waves with distinct magnetic modulation vectors, enabling a wide range of magnetic orders depending on their combination. Among them, topologically nontrivial spin textures, such as a magnetic skyrmion, have been extensively studied owing to the eme… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180401] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Satoru Ohgata and Satoru Hayami

Multi-Q magnets consist of superposed spin-density waves with distinct magnetic modulation vectors, enabling a wide range of magnetic orders depending on their combination. Among them, topologically nontrivial spin textures, such as a magnetic skyrmion, have been extensively studied owing to the eme…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180401] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [8] Quasiparticle properties below coherence onset in ${\mathrm{YbAl}}_{3}$ nanostructures
item.description:
Authors: Dale T. Lowder, Gage Eichman, Yuxin Wan, Karthik Rao, Ruiwen Xie, Hongbin Zhang, Debjoty Paul, Shouvik Chatterjee, Darrell G. Schlom, Kyle M. Shen, Emilia Morosan, and Douglas Natelson

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Dale T. Lowder, Gage Eichman, Yuxin Wan, Karthik Rao, Ruiwen Xie, Hongbin Zhang, Debjoty Paul, Shouvik Chatterjee, Darrell G. Schlom, Kyle M. Shen, Emilia Morosan, and Douglas Natelson

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of quasiparticles in a heavy fermion system relevant to ARPES.

Tags: #Kondo #heavy-fermions #spectroscopy

Abstract: Author(s): Dale T. Lowder, Gage Eichman, Yuxin Wan, Karthik Rao, Ruiwen Xie, Hongbin Zhang, Debjoty Paul, Shouvik Chatterjee, Darrell G. Schlom, Kyle M. Shen, Emilia Morosan, and Douglas Natelson Mesoscopic transport measurements are underexplored as probes of quasiparticles and their properties in correlated metals. The mixed valence compound ${\mathrm{YbAl}}_{3}$ exhibits a single-ion Kondo temperature of 670 K, while thermodynamic and transport properties (probed with specific heat, magne… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201101] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dale T. Lowder, Gage Eichman, Yuxin Wan, Karthik Rao, Ruiwen Xie, Hongbin Zhang, Debjoty Paul, Shouvik Chatterjee, Darrell G. Schlom, Kyle M. Shen, Emilia Morosan, and Douglas Natelson

Mesoscopic transport measurements are underexplored as probes of quasiparticles and their properties in correlated metals. The mixed valence compound YbAl3 exhibits a single-ion Kondo temperature of 670 K, while thermodynamic and transport properties (probed with specific heat, magnetic susceptibili…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201101] Published Fri May 01, 2026

item.title: [9] Single-crystal growth, structural characterization, and physical properties of a decorated square-kagome antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{KCu}}_{7}{\mathrm{TeO}}_{4}{({\mathrm{SO}}_{4})}_{5}\mathrm{Cl}$
item.description:
Authors: Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, Liran Wang, Lunhua He, Aifeng Wang, Yisheng Chai, Young Sun, Yi Cui, Frederic Hardy, Christoph Meingast, Weiqiang Yu, Xinrun Mi, Michael Merz, and Mingquan He

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, Liran Wang, Lunhua He, Aifeng Wang, Yisheng Chai, Young Sun, Yi Cui, Frederic Hardy, Christoph Meingast, Weiqiang Yu, Xinrun Mi, Michael Merz, and Mingquan He

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct material interest in kagome lattices and frustrated magnetism.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #kagome #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, Liran Wang, Lunhua He, Aifeng Wang, Yisheng Chai, Young Sun, Yi Cui, Frederic Hardy, Christoph Meingast, Weiqiang Yu, Xinrun Mi, Michael Merz, and Mingquan He The square-kagome lattice (SKL) antiferromagnet, composed of two-dimensional corner-sharing triangles, provides a prominent platform for studying frustrated magnetism. However, material realizations of the SKL remain scarce. Here, we report the single-crystal growth, structural characterization, mag… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134447] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingjing Jing, Andreas Eich, Yiqiu Liu, Liran Wang, Lunhua He, Aifeng Wang, Yisheng Chai, Young Sun, Yi Cui, Frederic Hardy, Christoph Meingast, Weiqiang Yu, Xinrun Mi, Michael Merz, and Mingquan He

The square-kagome lattice (SKL) antiferromagnet, composed of two-dimensional corner-sharing triangles, provides a prominent platform for studying frustrated magnetism. However, material realizations of the SKL remain scarce. Here, we report the single-crystal growth, structural characterization, mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134447] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026

Tags: #topological #surfacestates

Abstract: Author(s): Nikhlesh S. Mehta, Bikash Patra, Mona Garg, Ghulam Mohmad, Pooja Bhardwaj, Savita Chaudhary, Jaskaran Singh, Yogesh Singh, Bahadur Singh, and Goutam Sheet The development of superconducting data storage devices is one of the coveted goals for next-generation low-power electronics, and interaction between superconducting and nontrivial topological states can potentially be useful to achieve this goal. Here we show that the superconducting diboride ${\m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134529] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026

The development of superconducting data storage devices is one of the coveted goals for next-generation low-power electronics, and interaction between superconducting and nontrivial topological states can potentially be useful to achieve this goal. Here we show that the superconducting diboride OsB2…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134529] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Interplay between superconductivity and altermagnetism in disordered materials and heterostructures
item.description:
Authors: Rodrigo de las Heras, Tim Kokkeler, Stefan Ilić, Ilya V. Tokatly, and F. Sebastian Bergeret

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Rodrigo de las Heras, Tim Kokkeler, Stefan Ilić, Ilya V. Tokatly, and F. Sebastian Bergeret

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on altermagnetism and superconductivity, directly touching a core user interest in altermagnetic materials.

Tags: #altermagnet #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Rodrigo de las Heras, Tim Kokkeler, Stefan Ilić, Ilya V. Tokatly, and F. Sebastian Bergeret Superconductivity and altermagnetism are distinct states of matter, both characterized by zero net magnetization. The authors show here that when these two orders coexist in heterostructures, their interplay generates a finite magnetization texture in real space that reflects the symmetry of the altermagnet’s spin splitting in momentum space. This “proximity-induced magnetization” arises from singlet–triplet mixing when the superconducting order parameter varies in space, leading to nonlinear magnetoelectric effects driven by supercurrents, as well as tunable 0 – π transitions in Josephson junctions. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 144516] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rodrigo de las Heras, Tim Kokkeler, Stefan Ilić, Ilya V. Tokatly, and F. Sebastian Bergeret

Superconductivity and altermagnetism are distinct states of matter, both characterized by zero net magnetization. The authors show here that when these two orders coexist in heterostructures, their interplay generates a finite magnetization texture in real space that reflects the symmetry of the altermagnet’s spin splitting in momentum space. This “proximity-induced magnetization” arises from singlet–triplet mixing when the superconducting order parameter varies in space, leading to nonlinear magnetoelectric effects driven by supercurrents, as well as tunable 0–π transitions in Josephson junctions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144516] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay between superconductivity and altermagnetism in disordered materials and heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo de las Heras, Tim Kokkeler, Stefan Ilić, Ilya V. Tokatly, and F. Sebastian Bergeret
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Pressure-induced reentrant superconductivity in the misfit layered compound ${(\mathrm{SnS})}_{1.15}(\mathrm{Ta}{\mathrm{S}}_{2})$
item.description:
Authors: Chutong Zhang, Jiajia Feng, Xiao Tang, Xiangzhuo Xing, Na Zuo, Xiaolei Yi, Yan Meng, Xiaoran Zhang, Rajesh Kumar Ulaganathan, Raman Sankar, Xiaofeng Xu, Xin Chen, and Xiaobing Liu

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Chutong Zhang, Jiajia Feng, Xiao Tang, Xiangzhuo Xing, Na Zuo, Xiaolei Yi, Yan Meng, Xiaoran Zhang, Rajesh Kumar Ulaganathan, Raman Sankar, Xiaofeng Xu, Xin Chen, and Xiaobing Liu

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Electronic reconstruction in a layered chalcogenide related to TMDs; highly relevant to quasi-2D spectroscopy.

Tags: #superconductivity #TMD #vanderWaals

Abstract: Author(s): Chutong Zhang, Jiajia Feng, Xiao Tang, Xiangzhuo Xing, Na Zuo, Xiaolei Yi, Yan Meng, Xiaoran Zhang, Rajesh Kumar Ulaganathan, Raman Sankar, Xiaofeng Xu, Xin Chen, and Xiaobing Liu Misfit layered compounds are a class of natural van der Waals heterostructures that enable bulk realizations of quasi-two-dimensional electronic states. Using pressure as a tuning knob, the authors uncover here pressure-induced reentrant superconductivity in the prototypical misfit compound (SnS) 1 . 15 (TaS 2 ). They reveal an intimate connection between the reemergence of superconductivity and a pressure-driven electronic reconstruction, establishing pressure as an effective means to tune carrier topology and superconducting states in misfit systems. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L140506] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chutong Zhang, Jiajia Feng, Xiao Tang, Xiangzhuo Xing, Na Zuo, Xiaolei Yi, Yan Meng, Xiaoran Zhang, Rajesh Kumar Ulaganathan, Raman Sankar, Xiaofeng Xu, Xin Chen, and Xiaobing Liu

Misfit layered compounds are a class of natural van der Waals heterostructures that enable bulk realizations of quasi-two-dimensional electronic states. Using pressure as a tuning knob, the authors uncover here pressure-induced reentrant superconductivity in the prototypical misfit compound (SnS)1.15(TaS2). They reveal an intimate connection between the reemergence of superconductivity and a pressure-driven electronic reconstruction, establishing pressure as an effective means to tune carrier topology and superconducting states in misfit systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140506] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-induced reentrant superconductivity in the misfit layered compound ${(\mathrm{SnS})}_{1.15}(\mathrm{Ta}{\mathrm{S}}_{2})$
item.dc:creator: Chutong Zhang, Jiajia Feng, Xiao Tang, Xiangzhuo Xing, Na Zuo, Xiaolei Yi, Yan Meng, Xiaoran Zhang, Rajesh Kumar Ulaganathan, Raman Sankar, Xiaofeng Xu, Xin Chen, and Xiaobing Liu

item.title: [8] Evolution of magnetic correlation in the doped Hubbard model with altermagnetic spin splitting
item.description:
Authors: Yinlong Li, Rana Imran Mushtaq, Ji Liu, Wing Chi Yu, Xiaosen Yang, Cho-Tung Yip, and Ho-Kin Tang

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Yinlong Li, Rana Imran Mushtaq, Ji Liu, Wing Chi Yu, Xiaosen Yang, Cho-Tung Yip, and Ho-Kin Tang

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Discusses Fermi surface nesting in altermagnets; direct relevance to spectroscopy of altermagnetic materials.

Tags: #altermagnet #Fermisurface

Abstract: Author(s): Yinlong Li, Rana Imran Mushtaq, Ji Liu, Wing Chi Yu, Xiaosen Yang, Cho-Tung Yip, and Ho-Kin Tang The evolution of magnetic correlation in strongly correlated electron systems with altermagentic spin splitting remains largely unexplored. Here we investigate how spin splitting generated by spin-dependent next-nearest-neighbor hopping ${t}^{′}$ reshapes the Fermi surface nesting and Van Hove singu… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134443] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yinlong Li, Rana Imran Mushtaq, Ji Liu, Wing Chi Yu, Xiaosen Yang, Cho-Tung Yip, and Ho-Kin Tang

The evolution of magnetic correlation in strongly correlated electron systems with altermagentic spin splitting remains largely unexplored. Here we investigate how spin splitting generated by spin-dependent next-nearest-neighbor hopping t′ reshapes the Fermi surface nesting and Van Hove singularitie…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134443] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Evolution of magnetic correlation in the doped Hubbard model with altermagnetic spin splitting
item.dc:creator: Yinlong Li, Rana Imran Mushtaq, Ji Liu, Wing Chi Yu, Xiaosen Yang, Cho-Tung Yip, and Ho-Kin Tang

item.title: [7] Enhanced superconductivity driven by pressure-induced topological and structural transitions in ${\mathrm{Sn}}_{4}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$
item.description:
Authors: W. L. Zhu, R. Song, Z. W. Cheng, Q.-G. Mu, X.-R. Hu, Y. W. Zhang, J. H. Wang, Y. Z. Zhou, M. Zhao, H.-L. Cai, W. B. Kong, Y. Cao, Y. Y. Wang, J. Z. Gao, J. Li, L. Shan, P. Zhang, and M. H. Pan

Last/corresponding-author proxy: W. L. Zhu, R. Song, Z. W. Cheng, Q.-G. Mu, X.-R. Hu, Y. W. Zhang, J. H. Wang, Y. Z. Zhou, M. Zhao, H.-L. Cai, W. B. Kong, Y. Cao, Y. Y. Wang, J. Z. Gao, J. Li, L. Shan, P. Zhang, and M. H. Pan

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental tuning of superconductivity and topological states in a crystalline material.

Tags: #superconductivity #topological

Abstract: Author(s): W. L. Zhu, R. Song, Z. W. Cheng, Q.-G. Mu, X.-R. Hu, Y. W. Zhang, J. H. Wang, Y. Z. Zhou, M. Zhao, H.-L. Cai, W. B. Kong, Y. Cao, Y. Y. Wang, J. Z. Gao, J. Li, L. Shan, P. Zhang, and M. H. Pan Tuning the electronic states to understand the interplay between superconductivity and topological states is crucial for realizing topological quantum computation. High pressure provides a unique avenue for tuning both superconducting and topological states. Here, we report the pressure tuning of su… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134527] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): W. L. Zhu, R. Song, Z. W. Cheng, Q.-G. Mu, X.-R. Hu, Y. W. Zhang, J. H. Wang, Y. Z. Zhou, M. Zhao, H.-L. Cai, W. B. Kong, Y. Cao, Y. Y. Wang, J. Z. Gao, J. Li, L. Shan, P. Zhang, and M. H. Pan

Tuning the electronic states to understand the interplay between superconductivity and topological states is crucial for realizing topological quantum computation. High pressure provides a unique avenue for tuning both superconducting and topological states. Here, we report the pressure tuning of su…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134527] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhanced superconductivity driven by pressure-induced topological and structural transitions in ${\mathrm{Sn}}_{4}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$

item.title: [8] Microscopic theory of strain-controlled split superconducting and time-reversal symmetry breaking transitions in $s+id$ superconductors
item.description:
Authors: Anton Talkachov and Egor Babaev

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Anton Talkachov and Egor Babaev

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of time-reversal symmetry breaking in superconductors; highly relevant to user's TRSB interests.

Tags: #TRSB #Superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Anton Talkachov and Egor Babaev We study conditions of the appearance of $U(1)×{\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ superconducting states that spontaneously break time-reversal symmetry (BTRS) on a square lattice as a function of applied strain. Calculations show that if critical temperatures coincide at zero strain, they exhibit a linear kink and … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 144515] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anton Talkachov and Egor Babaev

We study conditions of the appearance of U(1)×Z2 superconducting states that spontaneously break time-reversal symmetry (BTRS) on a square lattice as a function of applied strain. Calculations show that if critical temperatures coincide at zero strain, they exhibit a linear kink and no kink otherwis…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144515] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic theory of strain-controlled split superconducting and time-reversal symmetry breaking transitions in $s+id$ superconductors

item.title: [7] Possibility of ferro-octupolar order in ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{2}{\mathrm{CaOsO}}_{6}$ assessed by x-ray magnetic dichroism measurements
item.description:
Authors: G. Shibata, N. Kawamura, J. Okamoto, A. Tanaka, H. Hayashi, K. Yamaura, H. Y. Huang, A. Singh, C. T. Chen, D. J. Huang, S. V. Streltsov, and A. Fujimori

Last/corresponding-author proxy: G. Shibata, N. Kawamura, J. Okamoto, A. Tanaka, H. Hayashi, K. Yamaura, H. Y. Huang, A. Singh, C. T. Chen, D. J. Huang, S. V. Streltsov, and A. Fujimori

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental spectroscopy of a 5d system with potential TRSB/multipole physics.

Tags: #Spectroscopy #TRSB

Abstract: Author(s): G. Shibata, N. Kawamura, J. Okamoto, A. Tanaka, H. Hayashi, K. Yamaura, H. Y. Huang, A. Singh, C. T. Chen, D. J. Huang, S. V. Streltsov, and A. Fujimori Localized $5{d}^{2}$ electrons in a cubic crystal field possess multipoles such as electric quadrupoles and magnetic octupoles. We studied the cubic double perovskite ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{2}{\mathrm{CaOsO}}_{6}$ containing the ${\mathrm{Os}}^{6+}$ ($5{d}^{2}$) ions, which exhibits a phase transition to a… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 165151] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. Shibata, N. Kawamura, J. Okamoto, A. Tanaka, H. Hayashi, K. Yamaura, H. Y. Huang, A. Singh, C. T. Chen, D. J. Huang, S. V. Streltsov, and A. Fujimori

Localized 5d2 electrons in a cubic crystal field possess multipoles such as electric quadrupoles and magnetic octupoles. We studied the cubic double perovskite Ba2CaOsO6 containing the Os6+ (5d2) ions, which exhibits a phase transition to a ‘‘hidden order’’ below T*∼ 50 K, by x-ray absorption spectr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165151] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Possibility of ferro-octupolar order in ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{2}{\mathrm{CaOsO}}_{6}$ assessed by x-ray magnetic dichroism measurements

item.title: [8] Field-induced antiferromagnetic transition in ${\mathrm{CeIrIn}}_{5}$
item.description:
Authors: Y. Tokunaga, M.-T. Suzuki, S. Krämer, H. Sakai, S. Kambe, H. Harima, D. Aoki, M. Horvatić, and I. Sheikin

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Y. Tokunaga, M.-T. Suzuki, S. Krämer, H. Sakai, S. Kambe, H. Harima, D. Aoki, M. Horvatić, and I. Sheikin

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of magnetic transitions in heavy-fermion materials.

Tags: #Magnetism #Correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Y. Tokunaga, M.-T. Suzuki, S. Krämer, H. Sakai, S. Kambe, H. Harima, D. Aoki, M. Horvatić, and I. Sheikin We report a low-temperature $^{115}\mathrm{In}$ nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) study of the prototypical heavy-fermion compound ${\mathrm{CeIrIn}}_{5}$ in high magnetic fields applied close to the crystallographic $c$ axis. For this orientation, a field-induced transition was previously reported t… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L140406] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Y. Tokunaga, M.-T. Suzuki, S. Krämer, H. Sakai, S. Kambe, H. Harima, D. Aoki, M. Horvatić, and I. Sheikin

We report a low-temperature In115 nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) study of the prototypical heavy-fermion compound CeIrIn5 in high magnetic fields applied close to the crystallographic c axis. For this orientation, a field-induced transition was previously reported to take place at about 28 T. Alth…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140406] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Field-induced antiferromagnetic transition in ${\mathrm{CeIrIn}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Y. Tokunaga, M.-T. Suzuki, S. Krämer, H. Sakai, S. Kambe, H. Harima, D. Aoki, M. Horvatić, and I. Sheikin

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Probing superconductivity with tunneling spectroscopy in rhombohedral graphene
item.description:
Authors: Denis Sedov and Mathias S. Scheurer

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Denis Sedov and Mathias S. Scheurer

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Tunneling spectroscopy (STM/STS) theory for superconducting/magnetic states in rhombohedral graphene.

Tags: #STS #Superconductivity #Graphene

Abstract: Author(s): Denis Sedov and Mathias S. Scheurer Probing the form of the order parameter of a superconductor is notoriously difficult. Here, the authors develop a general theoretical formalism to describe the tunneling current from a metal into a superconductor. They apply it to rhombohedral stacks of graphene, which were recently shown to host superconductivity emerging out of a normal state with some form of intrinsic magnetism. Multiple unusual tunneling features are predicted that could allow us to pinpoint the specific form of pairing in future tunneling experiments. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L140503] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Denis Sedov and Mathias S. Scheurer

Probing the form of the order parameter of a superconductor is notoriously difficult. Here, the authors develop a general theoretical formalism to describe the tunneling current from a metal into a superconductor. They apply it to rhombohedral stacks of graphene, which were recently shown to host superconductivity emerging out of a normal state with some form of intrinsic magnetism. Multiple unusual tunneling features are predicted that could allow us to pinpoint the specific form of pairing in future tunneling experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L140503] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Probing superconductivity with tunneling spectroscopy in rhombohedral graphene
item.dc:creator: Denis Sedov and Mathias S. Scheurer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Localized excitons and Landau-level mixing in time-reversal symmetric pairs of Chern bands
item.description:
Authors: Guopeng Xu, Nemin Wei, Inti Sodemann Villadiego, and Chunli Huang

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Guopeng Xu, Nemin Wei, Inti Sodemann Villadiego, and Chunli Huang

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of instabilities in Chern bands and moiré materials, relevant for topological electronic structure.

Tags: #Topological #Moiré #Chern

Abstract: Author(s): Guopeng Xu, Nemin Wei, Inti Sodemann Villadiego, and Chunli Huang Motivated by correlated phases observed in moiré transition metal dichalcogenide experiments, the authors study here how Coulomb interactions are modified and how they can drive many-body instabilities. Using a Landau level description, they show that interactions acquire a spin-dependent structure that can be systematically characterized by generalized Haldane pseudopotentials. They find that Landau level mixing can destabilize the Chern insulating state at integer filling when interactions become comparable to kinetic energy, providing an experimentally relevant mechanism for the breakdown of Chern insulators in moiré materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L161121] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guopeng Xu, Nemin Wei, Inti Sodemann Villadiego, and Chunli Huang

Motivated by correlated phases observed in moiré transition metal dichalcogenide experiments, the authors study here how Coulomb interactions are modified and how they can drive many-body instabilities. Using a Landau level description, they show that interactions acquire a spin-dependent structure that can be systematically characterized by generalized Haldane pseudopotentials. They find that Landau level mixing can destabilize the Chern insulating state at integer filling when interactions become comparable to kinetic energy, providing an experimentally relevant mechanism for the breakdown of Chern insulators in moiré materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161121] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Localized excitons and Landau-level mixing in time-reversal symmetric pairs of Chern bands
item.dc:creator: Guopeng Xu, Nemin Wei, Inti Sodemann Villadiego, and Chunli Huang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: [9] Spin-orbit torque switching of Néel order in band-inverted antiferromagnetic bilayer ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$

Authors: Rajibul Islam, Shakeel Ahmad, and Fei Xue

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Rajibul Islam, Shakeel Ahmad, and Fei Xue

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct focus on MnBi2Te4, a key magnetic topological material for the user.

Tags: #Topological #Antiferromagnet #MnBi2Te4

Abstract: Author(s): Rajibul Islam, Shakeel Ahmad, and Fei Xue Magnetic topological insulators host exotic phenomena such as the quantum anomalous Hall effect and quantized magnetoelectric responses, but dynamic electrical control of their topological phases remains elusive. Here we demonstrate from first principles that spin-orbit torque enables direct electri… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134440] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rajibul Islam, Shakeel Ahmad, and Fei Xue

Magnetic topological insulators host exotic phenomena such as the quantum anomalous Hall effect and quantized magnetoelectric responses, but dynamic electrical control of their topological phases remains elusive. Here we demonstrate from first principles that spin-orbit torque enables direct electri…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134440] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit torque switching of Néel order in band-inverted antiferromagnetic bilayer ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$

Last/corresponding-author proxy: M. M. Vasiakin, A. A. Manokhina, and A. S. Mel'nikov

Tags: #Altermagnet #Superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): M. M. Vasiakin, A. A. Manokhina, and A. S. Mel'nikov We present a microscopic derivation of Usadel-type theory describing the superconductor-altermagnet hybrid structures in the dirty limit. The combined influence of the momentum-dependent exchange field and potential scattering at nonmagnetic impurities is shown to result in the appearance of the eff… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 134526] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

The combined influence of the momentum-dependent exchange field and potential scattering at nonmagnetic impurities is shown to result in the appearance of the eff…

Abstract: Author(s): Clara K. Geschner, Adam Yanis Chaou, Vatsal Dwivedi, and Piet W. Brouwer A two-dimensional second-order topological insulator exhibits topologically protected zero-energy states at its corners. In the literature, the breathing kagome lattice with nearest-neighbor hopping is often mentioned as an example of a two-dimensional second-order topological insulator. Here, we sh… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 155151] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

A two-dimensional second-order topological insulator exhibits topologically protected zero-energy states at its corners. In the literature, the breathing kagome lattice with nearest-neighbor hopping is often mentioned as an example of a two-dimensional second-order topological insulator. Here, we sh…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155151] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

Leuenberger Flat-band breathing kagome lattices host strong Coulomb interactions that can fundamentally alter the nature of their low-energy degrees of freedom. Here we show, using first-principles Density Functional Theory-GW–Bethe-Salpeter theory, that single-layer ${\mathrm{Nb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cl}}_{8}$ realize… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 155153] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

Author(s): Mahtab A. Khan, Naseem Ud Din, Dmitry Skachkov, Dirk R. Englund, and Michael N. Leuenberger

Flat-band breathing kagome lattices host strong Coulomb interactions that can fundamentally alter the nature of their low-energy degrees of freedom. Here we show, using first-principles Density Functional Theory-GW–Bethe-Salpeter theory, that single-layer Nb3Cl8 realizes a spin-triplet Frenkel excit…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155153] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Shuo Zhang, Ying Liu, Zining Hu, Lei Jin, Xuefang Dai, Guodong Liu, and Xiaoming Zhang This work provides a comprehensive study of charge-2 Dirac points (C2 DPs) in spinless systems, systematically analyzing their emergence and properties based on the 230 space groups and corresponding material candidates. By deriving the necessary symmetry constraints, we establish that the stabiliza… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 155156] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

By deriving the necessary symmetry constraints, we establish that the stabiliza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155156] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
